nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal reef environments, rocky substrates, and sandy areas in the photic zone
nativeRange.biotope: Marine benthic; anchors via rhizoids into substrate; siphonous green macroalgae typical of warm, well-lit reef zones

avoidWith: tanks with no light schedule control

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product image from Mosaic Macros listing. Only known trade image for this unidentified Caulerpa sp.

summary: Lobe-Bladed Fern Caulerpa is an unidentified Caulerpa species sold by Mosaic Macros, recognizable by its succulent, thick lobed blades that resemble a fern frond. It grows quickly and is considered beginner-friendly, but produces allelopathic compounds and is banned for sale in California.
careNotes:
Lobe-Bladed Fern Caulerpa is a trade name applied by Mosaic Macros to an unidentified Caulerpa sp. with distinctive succulent, thick lobed blades described as growing in a pattern comparable to Caulerpa mexicana. No species-level identification has been published, so care guidance is drawn from genus-level Caulerpa standards. Mosaic Macros is the only known source in the hobby trade.

This algae is sold in cuttings of 2–3" (4–5 blades) or 4"+ (6+ blades). It is suitable for both display tank use as an ornamental specimen and refugium placement for nutrient export.

ALLELOPATHY: Like other Caulerpa species, this macroalgae is considered to produce secondary metabolites — chemical compounds, notably caulerpin and caulerpenyne — that have been documented to inhibit or stress nearby corals and competing algae. It is generally advised to maintain physical separation from sensitive SPS corals (the stony coral group most vulnerable to chemical stress), and running activated carbon in the system is a commonly recommended precaution. Refugium placement limits direct allelopathic exposure to display corals.

GOING SEXUAL / REPRODUCTIVE CRASH: Under stress — particularly from nutrient crashes, disrupted photoperiod, or rapid temperature swings — Caulerpa species can undergo holocarpy, a process in which the entire plant converts to reproductive gametes and effectively dies. This releases organic matter and gametes into the water column, creating a severe acute water-quality risk (ammonia spike, oxygen depletion) that is capable of crashing a reef tank. Recommended mitigations: run refugium lighting on a 24-hour cycle to suppress reproductive triggering; harvest regularly to keep the colony in active vegetative growth; maintain stable moderate nutrient levels rather than running nutrients to zero; immediately remove any specimen showing white or translucent fronds, or any sign of turbid gamete release.

INVASIVE CONCERN: The Caulerpa genus includes several documented marine invasive species, notably C. taxifolia in the Mediterranean and C. racemosa in various locations worldwide. All Caulerpa are banned for sale in California. Captive specimens must never be released into natural waterways.

WATER PARAMETERS: Standard reef parameters are generally advised — 22–26°C, salinity SG 1.023–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, alkalinity 8–12 dKH.

GENERAL CARE: Tends to thrive under high reef lighting (5,000K or higher, 8+ hours photoperiod, or 24-hour continuous for refugium use). Tolerates moderate water flow; gentle tumbling works well in a refugium chamber.

UPON ARRIVAL: Fronds may appear white or translucent after dark shipping due to chloroplast displacement. This is normal; coloration typically recovers within a few hours of light exposure.
breedingNotes: Like other Caulerpa species, this algae is generally considered to propagate easily via fragmentation — severed frond pieces have been reported to establish as new plants. Vegetative tip growth along the stolon (the horizontal runner from which fronds emerge) is the primary growth mode in captivity.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole known hobby trade source for this unidentified Caulerpa sp. Product page confirms succulent thick lobed blades, Caulerpa mexicana growth comparison, display suitability, California ban, and invasive designation. Specific water parameters and detailed husbandry not provided; extrapolated from genus-level Caulerpa standards.

additional.name: Caulerpa prolifera (sw-algae-004) — sibling entry
additional.notes: Cross-reference for genus-level husbandry standards: water parameters, allelopathy (caulerpin/caulerpenyne), holocarpy/crash behavior, invasive caveats, and propagation methods. Consistent with feather-caulerpa (sw-algae-035) sibling.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
